statementLines: Consider the following statements about ‘PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana’:
I. It targets installation of one crore solar rooftop panels in the residential sector.
II. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy aims to impart training on installation, operation, maintenance and repairs of solar rooftop systems at grassroot levels.
III. It aims to create more than three lakhs skilled manpower through fresh skilling, and upskilling, under scheme component of capacity building.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about ‘PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana’: I. It targets installation of one crore solar rooftop panels in the residential sector. II. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy aims to impart training on installation, operation, maintenance and repairs of solar rooftop systems at grassroot levels. III. It aims to create more than three lakhs skilled manpower through fresh skilling, and upskilling, under scheme component of capacity building. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **

Statement I is correct: **The**PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana**, launched in February 2024, is an ambitious initiative by the Government of India**aiming to install rooftop solar panels in one crore (10 million) households in the **residential sector by March 2027**. This large-scale adoption of solar energy is a significant step towards achieving energy self-reliance and promoting sustainable energy practices across the nation.**

Statement II is correct: **The**Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has emphasized the importance of **capacity building under this scheme. **It plans to**provide training at the grassroots level on the installation, **operation, maintenance, and repair of solar rooftop systems. **This initiative ensures that there is adequate skilled manpower to support the widespread adoption of solar energy.**

Statement III is correct: **A critical component of the scheme is the development of a skilled workforce to support the solar energy sector.**The objectives include: **-**Training Over 3 Lakh Individuals**: Through a combination of**fresh skilling and up-skilling programs.**-**Specialized Roles**: Out of these,**at least 1 lakh individuals will be trained as Solar PV Technicians**, specializing in photovoltaic systems.
These initiatives are part of the scheme’s capacity building component, aiming to empower individuals with the necessary skills to contribute to the solar energy ecosystem.

statementLines: In the context of forms of capital formation consider the following statements:
Statement I: The physical capital is separable from its owner, whereas, human capital is inseparable from its owner.
Statement II: Physical capital is an intangible asset whereas Human capital is tangible asset.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: In the context of forms of capital formation consider the following statements: Statement I: The physical capital is separable from its owner, whereas, human capital is inseparable from its owner. Statement II: Physical capital is an intangible asset whereas Human capital is tangible asset. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **

Statement I is correct: The owner of a physical capital, need not be present in the place where it is used. The **physical capital is separable from its owner, whereas, human capital is inseparable from its owner. **

Statement II is incorrect: Physical capital is a tangible**asset and can be easily sold in the market like any other commodity.**Human capital is intangible**; and it is endogenously built in the body and mind of its owner. Tangible assets are physical assets that can be seen, touched and felt. An intangible asset is a non-physical asset that cannot be seen or touched.

questions.questionText: With reference to human development across various States in India, which of the following statements is/are correct? I. Bihar has the highest Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) among all States in India. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Statement I is incorrect: **According to the latest data from the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI),** Karnataka (and not Haryana) has the highest per capita income amongst all States in India. **The top ten states/UTs in India with the highest per capita income (FY24-25) are**Karnataka (Rs 2,04,605), followed by Tamil **Nadu (Rs 1,96,309), Haryana (Rs 1,94,285), Telangana, Maharashtra, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Puducherry, **Andhra Pradesh and Punjab**. - As per the provisional estimates of GDP released by National Statistical Office, Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), the**per capita Net National Income (NNI) at constant prices for **2024-25 stands at**₹1,14,710.**

Statement II is incorrect: Mizoram (and not Kerala) has the highest literacy rate among all States in India. **Recently, the**state of Mizoram has achieved “full literacy” status with 98.2%, **surpassing the 95% literacy rate threshold set by the Ministry of Education.**

Statement III is incorrect: **Infant mortality rate (IMR) is defined as the number of deaths of children per 1,000 live births under one year. According to the**Sample Registration System (SRS) report for 2023**issued by the Registrar General of India,**Madhya Pradesh, Chattisgarh, and Uttar Pradesh reported the highest levels of IMR in the country **at 37, while IMR in Bihar was 27 per 1,000 live births. **The States of Manipur (at 3) followed by Kerala (at 5) recorded the lowest levels of IMR in the country. As of 2023, India’s overall IMR stands at 25 deaths per 1,000 live births. Knowledge Base:**Literacy Rate:**- According to the Office of the Registrar General of India,**a person aged 7 years and above who can read and **write with understanding in any language is considered literate.**- The total literacy rate of India, including all individuals (men and women), aged 7 and above, from rural and urban populations, accounts for 80.9% of the period**2023-24**.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is the best description of the term ‘unemployment trap’?
It refers to a situation where:
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is the best description of the term ‘unemployment trap’? It refers to a situation where:

options.a: A large number of job-seeking individuals are not able to find employment.
options.b: An individual remains in a job due to a lack of alternative employment opportunities.
options.c: Unemployment benefits discourage unemployed persons from going to work.
options.d: A person employed in a job contributes much less than his or her full capacity.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **

Option c is correct. Unemployment trap**is a situation in which**unemployment benefits discourage the unemployed **persons from going to work. **An unemployment trap arises when the opportunity cost of going to work is higher than the income received. This discourages people from going to work and being productive. It is a situation where an unemployed person who is receiving unemployment allowance is not encouraged to seek work/employment because his earnings as employed are lower than the benefits received while unemployed.

statementLines: In the context of the global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI), which of the given below is one of the indicators mentioned for calculating the index?
questions.questionText: In the context of the global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI), which of the given below is one of the indicators mentioned for calculating the index?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer **The Global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) is an international measure of acute poverty developed by the Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative (OPHI) and UNDP for inclusion in the 2010 Human Development Report. The MPI assesses poverty at the individual level. If a person is deprived of a third or more of ten (weighted) indicators, the global MPI identifies them as ‘MPI poor’. The extent – or intensity – of their poverty is also measured through the percentage of deprivations they are experiencing.**Sanitation is one of the indicators under the Multidimensional poverty index. **Options a, c and d are incorrect: **Life Expectancy at Birth is one of the indicator under Human Development Index. Antenatal care is one of the indicators under India’s National Multidimensional Poverty Index prepared by NITI Aayog. Monetary Poverty is an indicator under the Multidimensional Poverty Measure by the World Bank.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: In the context of macroeconomics of a country, Okun's Law describes the relationship between
questions.questionText: In the context of macroeconomics of a country, Okun's Law describes the relationship between

options.c: Unemployment and Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth rate.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Based on the empirical research of Arthur Okun (1928–80),**Okun's law describes the relationship between **unemployment and GDP growth rate in an economy**. According to it, if GDP grows at 3 per cent per annum, the unemployment rate would not change. In the case of faster growth rates,**every extra above the 3 per cent will have a **decrease in the unemployment rate by half** (i.e., a 4 per cent growth rate will decrease unemployment by 0.5 per cent – half of 1 which is the extra above 3 per cent). Similarly, a growth rate below 3 per cent will have the same but opposite impact on unemployment by increasing the unemployment rate.
Though the law was perfectly correct for the period of the US economy Okun studied, it may not be valid today in either the US or anywhere else. But in general, the law is still used by experts and policymakers as a rule of thumb to estimate the relationship between growth rate and job creation.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **In Census 2011,**literacy is defined as the ability of a person aged seven years and above to read and write with **understanding in any language**. For a state to declare itself "fully literate," every household must be surveyed, all remaining non-literate adults enrolled in literacy classes, and results verified at multiple levels. In this context, government of India launched ULLAS-Nav Bharat Saaksharta Karyakram(New India Literacy Programme (NILP)) in 2020 with the aim of empowering adults aged 15 and above from all backgrounds who missed formal schooling, helping them integrate into society and contribute to the nation ‘s growth.**Under the scheme, a **state is considered fully literate when its literacy rate exceeds 95%. **According to the assessment, Mizoram was the first state to be declared fully literate under the ULLAS scheme, **followed by Goa, Tripura, and Himachal Pradesh. **Additionally, Ladakh became the first Union Territory to achieve full literacy status under the programme.

statementLines: In the context of Indian economy, consider the following statements:
I. The unemployment rate in the economy may be zero if the Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) is equal to the
Worker Population Ratio (WPR).
II. In the last 5 years, annually, the unemployment rate in urban areas has always been higher than rural areas.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: In the context of Indian economy, consider the following statements: I. The unemployment rate in the economy may be zero if the Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) is equal to the Worker Population Ratio (WPR). II. In the last 5 years, annually, the unemployment rate in urban areas has always been higher than rural areas. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **

Statement I is correct: **As per the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), the**Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR)**measures the percentage of the population that is in the labour force, i.e., those who are employed, seeking employment, or available for work. The**Worker Population Ratio (WPR)**measures the percentage of the population that is actually employed. The**Unemployment Rate (UR) **is defined as the proportion of persons in the labour force who are unemployed and is calculated as: UR= (Number of unemployed persons / labour force) *100.**From this relationship, when LFPR equals WPR, the number of unemployed persons becomes zero, and therefore **the unemployment rate is zero. **For an unemployed person to be counted in the Labour Force, s/he must meet three specific criteria: - Be without work: They did not have a job during the survey reference period. - Be currently available for work: They were available to start working. - Be actively seeking work: They made specific efforts to find a job within the recent past Thus, those not seeking work will not be counted as part of the labour force.**

Statement II is correct: As is clear from the figure given below that between **2019-20**20 and **2023-24**, the **unemployment rate in urban areas has always been higher than rural areas. **

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: If value of Gini coefficient of a country is increasing consistently over a decade, it indicates:
questions.questionText: If value of Gini coefficient of a country is increasing consistently over a decade, it indicates:

options.a: Income inequality in the economy is continuously increasing.
options.b: The level of extreme poverty in the country is decreasing.
options.c: Income distribution is becoming more equal over time in an economy.
options.d: The standard of living of all sections of the population is improving.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The Gini Coefficient is a widely used statistical measure to assess income or wealth inequality in an economy. It is calculated as**the ratio of the area between the Lorenz Curve and the line of perfect equality to the total area under **the line of perfect equality**. The Gini Coefficient measures the degree of inequality in the distribution of income or wealth within a country or states. The value of the Gini Coefficient ranges from 0 to 1, where 0 represents perfect equality and 1 represents perfect inequality. An increasing Gini Coefficient indicates rising income inequality, irrespective of changes in the absolute income **levels of the population.**

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Consider the following pairs:
Term Description
I. Infant Mortality Rate Death of children under the age of five years per 1000 live births in a particular year.
II. Maternal Mortality Rate Number of women who die as a result of complications of pregnancy or childbearing in a given year per 100,000 live births in that year.
III. Incidence Rate Number of deaths per 1000 persons in a given population in a year.
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.questionText: Consider the following pairs: Term Description I. Infant Mortality Rate Death of children under the age of five years per 1000 live births in a particular year. II. Maternal Mortality Rate Number of women who die as a result of complications of pregnancy or childbearing in a given year per 100,000 live births in that year. III. Incidence Rate Number of deaths per 1000 persons in a given population in a year. Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Pair I is incorrect: Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) is the number of deaths of infants before reaching the age of one **(and not the age of five),**in a particular year, per 1,000 live births during that year. On the other hand, child mortality refers to the total number of child deaths under the age of five years per 1000 live births.**Pair II is correct: **Maternal mortality refers to death that occurs due to complications during pregnancy and childbirth.**Maternal Mortality Rate (MMR)**is the**relationship between the number of maternal deaths due to **childbearing by the number of live births**or by the sum of live births and fetal deaths in a given year. It is defined as the**number of maternal deaths during a given time period per 100,000 live births **during the same time period. If a woman is pregnant or dies within 42 days of termination of pregnancy, it is also referred to as maternal mortality.**Pair III is incorrect:**Incidence Rate measures the annual incidence of a disease.**The numerator of the rate is the number of times attacks of a certain disease are reported in a year, and the denominator is the population exposed to the disease during the same year. The rate can exceed 1, as there could be repeated attacks of the disease to the same person. Knowledge Base:**Other Related Terms:**-**Mortality rate:**It is the annual number of deaths (from a disease or in general) per 1,000 people. -**Morbidity rate:**It refers to the number of people who have a disease compared to the total number of people in a population. -**Crude Death rate (CDR): **It is the simplest measure of the general level of mortality in a population. It gives the number of deaths that occur, on the average, per 1,000 people in the community. -**Birth rate:** It is a crude measure of fertility of a population and is a crucial determinant of population growth. It gives the number of live births per thousand population in a given region and year.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: A handicraft factory introduced automated machines which could mass produce handicrafts and create them within minutes with different designs. As a result, many workers who had the expertise in handmade handicrafts lost their jobs and now struggle to find new employment, as most of these modern factories now require skills in operating and maintaining automated machinery.
Which type of unemployment is being described in the above passage?
questions.questionText: A handicraft factory introduced automated machines which could mass produce handicrafts and create them within minutes with different designs. As a result, many workers who had the expertise in handmade handicrafts lost their jobs and now struggle to find new employment, as most of these modern factories now require skills in operating and maintaining automated machinery. Which type of unemployment is being described in the above passage?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **

Option a is correct: **The** situation described highlights a permanent change in the structure of the industry **due to technological advancement. The handicraft factory introduced automated machines which replaced manual labour. This matches the definition of**structural unemployment**, which arises when: - the structure of the economy changes, such as through technological advancement or modernization, - workers become unemployed because their existing skills become obsolete, and-new jobs require skills that the displaced workers do not possess, creating a mismatch between skills demanded and skills available.**

Option b is incorrect: Disguised unemployment **occurs**when more people are employed than required to produce a **given level of output**. It is most common in agriculture, where even if a few workers withdraw, total production does not fall because their contribution is marginal or zero. 

Option c is incorrect: Cyclical unemployment**arises due to** economic recessions or slowdowns**, when**firms reduce **output**because overall demand in the economy falls. It is linked to business cycles, which are periods of boom and recession. When demand picks up again, jobs are usually restored. 

Option d is incorrect: Frictional unemployment**is**short-term, temporary unemployment**. It occurs when**workers **are between jobs, are searching for better opportunities, and have left a job voluntarily**. These workers still possess skills that are useful and in demand; they are simply taking time to find a new job.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Consider the following statements with regard to provisions of The Code on Wages, 2019:
I. It establishes a statutory right to minimum wages for all employees across both organized and unorganized sectors.
II. Employers must pay all employees overtime wages at least twice the normal rate for any work done beyond the regular working hours.
III. It provides scope for regional variation and gives power to states who can fix a minimum wage below the floor wage set by the Centre under the code.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements with regard to provisions of The Code on Wages, 2019: I. It establishes a statutory right to minimum wages for all employees across both organized and unorganized sectors. II. Employers must pay all employees overtime wages at least twice the normal rate for any work done beyond the regular working hours. III. It provides scope for regional variation and gives power to states who can fix a minimum wage below the floor wage set by the Centre under the code. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The**Code on Wages, 2019**is**one of the four labour codes**enacted to simplify, rationalize, and consolidate labour laws in India. It**replaces and integrates provisions of four existing laws:**the**Payment of Wages Act, 1936**, the**Minimum **Wages Act, 1948**, the**Payment of Bonus Act, 1965**, and the**Equal Remuneration Act, 1976**. It aims to strengthen workers’ rights while promoting simplicity and uniformity in wage-related compliance for employers. The**other three labour codes are: ****The Code on Social Security, 2020**– dealing with social security, welfare benefits, and social insurance. **The Industrial Relations Code, 2020**– dealing with trade unions, industrial disputes, and employment conditions. **The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (OSH) Code, 2020**– covering safety, health, and working conditions across establishments.**

Statement I is correct:**The**Code on Wages, 2019 establishes a statutory right to minimum wages for all employees**,**covering both organized and unorganized sectors**. Earlier, the Minimum Wages Act applied only to scheduled employment covering ~30% of workers.**

Statement II is correct:**Section 14**of the**Code on Wages, 2019 **, states that, where an employee whose minimum rate of wages has been fixed under this Code by the hour, by the day or by such a longer wage-period as may be prescribed, works on any day in excess of the number of hours constituting a normal working day,**the employer shall **pay him for every hour or for part of an hour so worked in excess, at the overtime rate which shall not be less than **twice the normal rate of wages**. Thus, employers must pay all employees overtime wages at least twice the normal rate for any work done beyond the regular working hours.**

Statement III is incorrect: **The**Code provides for a statutory floor wage**, but this floor wage is**fixed by the Central **Government**based on minimum living standards,**not the respective State Governments.** States can fix minimum wages, but these cannot be lower than the central floor wage, ensuring uniformity and adequacy nationwide.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: With reference to casual workers employed in India, consider the following statements:
All casual workers are entitled for Employees Provident Fund Coverage.
All casual workers are entitled for regular working hours and overtime payment.
The government can by a notification specify that an establishment or industry shall pay wages only through its bank account.
Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to casual workers employed in India, consider the following statements: 1. All casual workers are entitled for Employees Provident Fund Coverage. 2. All casual workers are entitled for regular working hours and overtime payment. 3. The government can by a notification specify that an establishment or industry shall pay wages only through its bank account. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **

Statement 1 is incorrect.**A casual worker is a worker on a temporary employment contract with generally limited entitlements to benefits and little or no security of employment. The main attribute is the absence of a continuing relationship of any stability with an employer, which could lead to their not being considered ‘employees’ at all.**Note**: Though Supreme Court had ruled that**casual workers are also entitled to social security benefits under the **Employees’ Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act.**“As per Section 2(f) of the EPF Act, the definition of an employee is an inclusive definition, and is widely worded to include any person engaged either directly or indirectly in connection with the work of an establishment, and is paid wages,” a bench of SC said.**But the word ‘All’ makes the **statement extreme and would not be true always thus the statement is incorrect. **

Statement 2 is correct.**Casual workers (should) have all those rights that are enjoyed by regular workers, since the definition of employee includes casual labour as per Supreme Court guidelines. Employees in India**are entitled for **regular working hours and overtime payment according to Minimum Wages Rules, 1950.**

Statement 3 is correct.**The Payment of Wages (Amendment) Act 2017 provides that the Government may specify that the employer of any industrial or other establishment**shall pay wages to every employee**only by cheque or by **crediting the wages in his bank account**”.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: In the context of macroeconomic theory, the Phillips Curve illustrates:
questions.questionText: In the context of macroeconomic theory, the Phillips Curve illustrates:

options.a: A proportional relationship between household income and expenditure on a particular good in an economy.
options.b: A proportional relationship between inflation rate and fiscal deficit in an economy.
options.c: An inverse relationship between unemployment rate and rate of inflation in an economy.
options.d: An inverse relationship between wage rate and labour productivity rate in an economy.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer**. The**Phillips Curve**, developed by A.W. Phillips, shows an**inverse relationship**between the**rate of unemployment **and the**rate of inflation**. According to the curve, when unemployment is low, inflation tends to be high due to increased demand for goods and labour. Conversely, high unemployment generally leads to lower inflation as demand slows down. This trade-off forms the**basis of the original Phillips Curve**, which is**downward sloping**in the short run. Over time, economists like Milton Friedman argued that this relationship does**not hold in the long run**, especially due to adaptive expectations.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following best describes the term ‘casualisation of workforce’? government. towards entrepreneurship.
questions.questionText: Which of the following best describes the term ‘casualisation of workforce’? government. towards entrepreneurship.

options.a: It reflects the workforce engaged in productive activities which are neither taxed nor registered with the
options.b: It signifies a shift in the workforce from permanent salaried jobs to self-employment due to government push
options.c: It reflects the transitional unemployment in certain sectors due to shifts in the economy.
options.d: It signifies shift in the workforce from regular salaried employment and self-employment towards casual workers.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer**. Casualisation means a growing number of workers are taking up jobs that are**temporary**,**insecure**, and**without any **social security benefits**. These are jobs where workers are hired on a day-to-day basis and can be removed anytime, without notice or compensation. This process involves a shift away from more secure forms of employment like**self
- **employment**or**regular salaried jobs **towards**casual wage work**, which is usually found in the**informal or **unorganised sector**. Knowledge Base: NITI Aayog released a report titled**“India’s Booming Gig and Platform Economy”**in June 2022. It is the**first comprehensive official study**on the size, composition, and future potential of India’s gig and platform workforce. The gig and platform economy consists of workers who earn livelihoods through**short-term, task-based, or **platform-mediated work**, often enabled by digital technologies such as apps, internet platforms, and smartphones.**Key Data on Gig Workers in India **Present Size (2020–21) **-**Total gig workers:**77 lakh (7.7 million)**-**Share of workforce:**○**2.6%**of non-agricultural workforce ○**1.5%**of total workforce in India**Projected Size (2029–30)
- nearly a three-fold increase **-**Expected gig workers:**2.35 crore (23.5 million)**-**Share of workforce:**○**6.7%**of non-agricultural workforce ○**4.1%**of total workforce / livelihoods**Skill Composition of Gig Workers (Present) **-**Medium-skilled jobs:**~**47%**-**Low-skilled jobs:**~**31%**-**High-skilled jobs:**~**22%**Observed Trend **- Share of**medium-skilled**gig work is**declining**- Share of**low-skilled and high-skilled**work is**increasing**
This reflects:
- Expansion of delivery, logistics, and service work (low skill)
- Growth of IT, design, consulting, and professional platforms (high skill)

(d) Neither Statement II nor statement III is correct
questions.questionText: In the context of India’s population growth, consider the following statements: Statement I: The year 1921 is known as the "Year of Great Divide". Statement II: The year 1921 distinguishes the previous era of fluctuating population growth to a phase of moderately increasing population growth. Statement III: The census of 1921 was the first census which recorded a positive decadal growth compared to the earlier census. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the statements given above?

options.a: Both statement II and statement III are correct and both of them explain statement I
options.b: Both statement II and statement III are correct, but only one of them explains statement I
options.c: Only one of the statements II and III is correct, and that explains statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor statement III is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer**. The history of India’s population growth reveals three broad demographic phases, with**1921, 1951, and 1981**serving as natural dividing points. Among these, the year**1921 stands out as the first major turning point**, marking a shift in the pattern of population growth in the country.**

Statement I is correct. **1921 is called the “**Year of Great Divide**” because it separates the earlier period of**fluctuating **and unstable population trends**from a new phase of**steady and continuous population growth**.**

Statement II is correct. **From 1871 to 1921, population growth in India was uneven and often interrupted by events like famines, epidemics, and high death rates. The 1921 Census recorded an**absolute decline in population**, making it the**only census**to show such a fall. From 1921 to 1951, the population began to**increase steadily**, adding**over 10 crore **people**in thirty years. The growth, though moderate compared to later decades, was consistent, with annual rates ranging between**1.0% and 1.35%**.**Statement II explains Statement I. The**shift from erratic to stable population growth**is exactly what gives 1921 its unique status. The year distinguishes the**pre-1921 era of chequered demographic behaviour**from the**post-1921 **phase of regular and accelerating growth**, hence justifying the term “Great Divide.”**

Statement III is incorrect: Between 1911 and 1921 there was a negative rate of growth of – 0.03%. This was because **of the influenza epidemic during 1918–19 which killed about 12.5 million persons **or 5% of the total population of the country. Thus it would be incorrect to say that census 1921 recorded a positive **decadal growth. **

source.sourceText: https://www.egyankosh.ac.in/bitstream/123456789/101231/1/Unit-5.pdf - Growth of Population of India – Page 72 https://ncert.nic.in/textbook/pdf/lesy102.pdf

questions.questionText: With reference to demography, which of the following best describes the term Gross Reproduction Rate (GRR)? period conforming to the age-specific Fertility Rates for a given year, if there is no mortality. reproductive span conforming to the age-specific Fertility Rates for a given year, if there is no mortality. reproductive period, conforming to the age-specific Fertility Rates for a given year, if there is no mortality.

options.a: It measures the average number of children expected to be born per woman during her entire span of reproductive
options.b: It measures the average number of female children a woman is expected to give birth during her entire
options.c: It measures the average number of children expected to be born per married woman during the entire span of her
options.d: It measures the number of live births per thousand women in the reproductive age group 15-49 years.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer**.**Gross Reproduction Rate (GRR) measures the average number of female children a woman is expected to give birth **during her entire reproductive span conforming to the age-specific Fertility Rates for a given year, if there is no **mortality. **It tells us how effectively women are**replacing themselves with daughters**in the next generation. The GRR does**not take into account any deaths, that**is, it assumes that every woman survives the full childbearing age.**

Option a is incorrect**. The average number of children expected to be born per woman during her entire span of reproductive period conforming to the age-specific Fertility Rates for a given year, if there is no mortality refers to the**Total Fertility Rate (TFR)**, not the Gross Reproduction Rate. The TFR calculates the**average number of children (both **boys and girls)**that a woman is expected to bear during her reproductive years, assuming she experiences the current pattern of age-specific fertility.**

Option c is incorrect**.** Total Marital Fertility Rate **measures the average number of children expected to be born per married woman during the entire span of her reproductive period, conforming to the age-specific Fertility Rates for a given year, if there is no mortality. 

Option d is incorrect**.**General Fertility Rate**measures the number of live births per thousand women in the reproductive age group 15-49 years. Knowledge Base:**Feature **Total Fertility Rate (TFR) **Gross Reproduction Rate (GRR) **Definition**Average number of children (both sons and daughters) a woman would have during her reproductive years Average number of**daughters**a woman would have during her reproductive years**Focus**Includes**all children**born to a woman Includes**only daughters**born to a woman**Purpose**Measures overall fertility level in a population Measures**female-based **population **replacement**potential**Link to Population **Replacement**TFR of about 2.1 is considered replacement level (in developed countries) GRR of**1.0** indicates exact replacement of mothers by daughters

statementLines: Consider the categories of employment:

questions.questionText: Consider the categories of employment: I. Self-employed II. Regular wage/salaried III. Casual labour Which of the following is the correct ascending order for the total number of persons employed in different categories of employment in urban areas as per the recent annual Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) report?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer**. As per the**data for latest annual Periodic Labour force survey i.e. (PLFS-**2023-24**)**-**Casual labour (III)**accounted for**12.1%**of total employment. -**Self-employed (I)**made up**40.4%**. -**Regular wage/salaried workers (II)**formed the largest share, at**47.5%**. Therefore, the increasing order of employment shares in urban areas is: III. Casual labour I. Self-employed II. Regular salaried Knowledge Base:**I.**Self-employed:**Self-employed persons are those who operate their own enterprises or businesses**. They do not work under a fixed employer and may work independently or with a few hired workers. Examples include street vendors, small shop owners, freelancers, and home-based workers. In urban India, they form a significant part of the informal sector and often lack social security benefits.**II. Regular wage/salaried:**These are individuals who work for others and receive a fixed wage or salary at regular intervals (monthly, weekly, **etc.).**They are generally employed in formal jobs such as those in government offices, private companies, educational institutions, etc. This category enjoys more**job security**and better working conditions compared to other types of employment.**III. Casual labour:**Casual labourers are those who work on a daily basis or for short durations**. They are often engaged in construction work, loading-unloading activities, or similar low-skill, physical jobs. These workers do**not have job security**, formal contracts, or regular wages. Their employment is unpredictable and entirely dependent on demand for labour.

statementLines: In the context of poverty estimation in India, consider the following statements:
Statement I: In 2022-23 there has been a significant drop in poverty estimates compared to 2011-12.
Statement II: The Household Consumption Expenditure Survey (HCES) 2023-2024 for the first time adopted Uniform recall period method to collect information related to household consumption.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: In the context of poverty estimation in India, consider the following statements: Statement I: In 2022-23 there has been a significant drop in poverty estimates compared to 2011-12. Statement II: The Household Consumption Expenditure Survey (HCES) 2023-2024 for the first time adopted Uniform recall period method to collect information related to household consumption. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **

Statement I is correct and II is incorrect: **India’s latest Household Consumption Expenditure Survey (HCES) adopted the Modified Mixed Recall Period **(MMRP) method, replacing the outdated Uniform Reference Period (URP). **The consumption recorded in national surveys rose, leading to a drop in poverty estimates: **- In 2011–12, applying MMRP reduced India’s poverty rate from 22.9% to 16.22%, even under the older $2.15 poverty line. - In 2022–23, poverty under the new $3.00 line stood at 5.25%, while under the older $2.15 line it dropped further to 2.35%. Note: The World Bank has announced a major revision to global poverty estimates, raising the International Poverty Line (IPL) from $2.15/day (2017 PPP) to $3.00/day (2021 PPP)**

Knowledge Base: ** Modified Mixed reference (Recall) Period (MMRP):
- Under MMRP, (a) 365-day recall period is used for clothing, footwear, education, institutional medical care and durable goods
(b) 7-day recall period for edible oil, egg, fish and meat, vegetable, fruits, spices, beverages, refreshment, processed food, paan, tobacco and intoxicants and (c) 30-day recall period for the remaining food items, fuel and light, miscellaneous goods and services including non-institutional medical, rents and taxes.
Uniform Reference (Recall) Period (URP):
- Under URP, consumption data for all items are collected for a 30-day recall period. When URP is applied, the households are surveyed about their consumption in the last 30 days preceding the date of survey.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: One of the earliest estimations of the poverty line in India using the concept of the ‘jail cost of living’ was made by:
questions.questionText: One of the earliest estimations of the poverty line in India using the concept of the ‘jail cost of living’ was made by:
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.**Dadabhai Naoroji in his book, “Poverty and unBritish Rule in India”**made one of the earliest systematic estimations of **the poverty in India**. His poverty estimate was based on the cost of a subsistence-level minimum diet, calculated using prevailing prices. He**used the dietary scale of prisoners**to arrive at what is often described as the ‘**jail cost of living**’, and appropriately adjusted this estimate to account for children, thereby approximating a poverty line. Dadabhai Naoroji was among the earliest Indian economists to systematically discuss the concept of a poverty line in pre-independent India. He also propounded the theory of the ‘**Drain of Wealth**’, highlighting the economic exploitation of India under British colonial rule. His work significantly influenced later economic thought and policy debates in India.

source.sourceText: NCERT Class XIth Indian Economic Development Chapter 4 - Poverty, Page: 63

**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **

Option a is incorrect. **Occasionally Poor are those**who are non-poor most of the time**, but may occasionally fall below the poverty line due to temporary shortages of income. 

Option b is incorrect. **Usually poor are those** who are poor most of the time**, but may sometimes earn enough to move out of poverty temporarily (for example, casual labourers).

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not correct about the Mother’s Absolute Affection Programme? 1. Allows all pregnant women to absolutely free and no-expense delivery in public health institutions. 2. Provides assured, comprehensive and quality antenatal care. 3. Promotes undiluted focus on breastfeeding. 4. Provides medical and nutritional care to severely acute malnourished children. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **The**Mother’s Absolute Affection (MAA) Programme **is a significant initiative launched by the**Ministry of Health and **Family Welfare in 2016, **under India’s**National Health Mission**, primarily**aimed at promoting optimal breastfeeding **practices **to improve maternal and child health.**

Statement 1 is incorrect: **While the MAA Programme focuses on breastfeeding, the**provision of free deliveries is **mainly covered under other schemes **such as the**Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)**, which aims to promote institutional deliveries by providing cash incentives to pregnant women.**

Statement 2 is incorrect: **The**MAA’s primary focus is on enhancing breastfeeding practices rather than directly **providing comprehensive antenatal services**. The antenatal care aspect is addressed by various other health initiatives, including the**Reproductive Maternal Newborn Child Adolescent Health Plus Nutrition (RMNCAH+N) **strategy.**

Statement 3 is correct: **The**MAA Programme **specifically**aims to promote early initiation of breastfeeding, **exclusive breastfeeding for the first six month**s, and appropriate complementary feeding thereafter. It emphasizes community awareness and training of ASHA and healthcare workers to support breastfeeding efforts.**

Statement 4 is incorrect: MAA does not aim to provide medical and nutritional care to severely acute malnourished **children**.**T**he provision of medical and nutritional care for severely malnourished children is typically handled through**Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs)**, which are separate from the MAA initiative. These NRCs focus on treating children under five years suffering from severe acute malnutrition with medical complications.

questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about India’s performance highlighted in the Human Development Report of United Nations Development Programme: I. India’s ranking in the Human Development Index has consistently improved over the past decade. II. As per Human Development Report 2025, India is in the ‘medium’ human development category. III. As per Human Development Report 2025, India’s Human Development Index (HDI) value has increased by more than 50% since 1990. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) released its 2025 Human Development Report (HDR) titled “A matter of choice: People and possibilities in the age of AI.” Human Development Reports (HDRs) have been released since 1990.**

Statement I is incorrect. **On the latest Human Development Index (HDI) - 2025, India was ranked 130 out of 193 countries. Although India’s HDI ranking has shown some improvement in recent years the improvement has**not been consistent **year-on-year over the last decade**. There have been fluctuations as can be seen from the table given below:**Year **India’s Ranking in Human **Development Index **2015 130 2016 131 2017 131 2018 129 2019 131 2022 133 2023 130**

Statement II is correct. **With its HDI value increasing from 0.676 in 2022 to 0.685 in 2023,** India remains in the **‘medium’ human development category. **It has thus moved closer to the threshold for high human development (HDI ≥ 0.700). According to UNDP, the cutoff-points of classification of countries based on HDI value are “HDI of less than 0.550 for low human development, 0.550–0.699 for medium human development, 0.700–0.799 for high human development and 0.800 or greater for very high human development.”**

Statement III is correct.**The report noted that India’s HDI value has increased by over 53% since 1990,** showing a growth faster than both the global and South Asian averages.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The sum total of which of the following types of unemployment constitutes the natural rate of unemployment?
I. Seasonal unemployment
II. Structural unemployment
III. Frictional unemployment
IV. Cyclical unemployment
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: The sum total of which of the following types of unemployment constitutes the natural rate of unemployment? I. Seasonal unemployment II. Structural unemployment III. Frictional unemployment IV. Cyclical unemployment Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The**natural rate of unemployment**represents the unemployment that exists even when the economy is producing at its full potential (i.e., full-employment level of output, or long-run equilibrium). This unemployment is**not due to **economic slowdown**but due to normal, unavoidable frictions in the labour market.**It consists only of structural and **frictional unemployment. ** Option I is incorrect: Seasonal Unemployment is not part of natural unemployment. **This occurs when employment is not available throughout the year, and workers have jobs only during certain seasons. It is**not counted in the natural rate of unemployment**, as it depends on seasonal cycles rather than the economy operating at full potential.  Option II is correct:**Structural Unemployment is included in the natural rate of unemployment. **This arises due to long-term structural changes in the economy; such as technological advancement, changes in industry composition, or mismatch between workers’ skills and job requirements. Since these factors persist even when the economy is at full potential**, structural unemployment is a core component of the natural rate of unemployment.** Option III is correct:**Frictional unemployment is included in the natural rate of unemployment. **This arises when individuals are temporarily between jobs, searching for new employment, or transitioning from education to work. Because such movement continues even in a healthy economy,**frictional unemployment is always present and thus **part of the natural rate of unemployment. ** Option IV is incorrect: Cyclical unemployment is not part of natural unemployment. **This arises due to economic downturns or recessions, when demand for goods and labour falls. This type of unemployment is temporary and linked to the business cycle. When the economy reaches full potential output, cyclical unemployment becomes zero, so**it is not part of the **natural rate of unemployment.**

statementLines: The Environmental Kuznets Curve (EKC) hypothesis states that: adopted. income is reached.
questions.questionText: The Environmental Kuznets Curve (EKC) hypothesis states that: adopted. income is reached.

options.a: Environmental resources are scarce and modern technologies must be used to allocate them efficiently.
options.b: Environmental degradation will inevitably exceed economic growth unless sustainable consumption practices are
options.c: Higher economic growth invariably leads to higher income inequality and environmental degradation.
options.d: Environmental degradation increases in the initial stages of economic growth but declines after a certain level of

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **

Option d is correct: **The** Environmental Kuznets Curve (EKC)**illustrates an inverted-U shaped relationship between economic growth and environmental degradation.**Environmental degradation initially increases, but after reaching **a threshold income level, it begins to decline**. EKC depicts the economy’s evolution from a clean agrarian stage to a polluting industrial stage, and eventually to a cleaner service-oriented stage. The logic behind the EKC is that in the early stages of economic growth, societies prioritize material output and income over environmental quality. At this stage, awareness of environmental problems is low, and environment-friendly production technologies are either unavailable or not widely adopted. Economic expansion, particularly through agriculture and resource-intensive industries, exerts enormous pressure on the environment, leading to higher pollution levels.**Beyond the threshold level of income, rising prosperity encourages people to value environmental quality**. This leads to structural changes in the economy, such as the adoption of less resource-intensive production methods, stricter enforcement of environmental regulations, and increased use of cleaner technologies, resulting in a decline in environmental degradation.

questions.questionText: Consider the following: I. Imparting education to students II. Purchasing of machines and equipment III. Providing pensions to old age people IV. Providing on-the job training to workers The expenditure made on how many of the above would contribute to human capital formation?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Human capital**refers to the knowledge, skills and abilities embodied in people that helps them to be productive. Human capital provides the ability to people to generate material wealth for an economy.**Human capital formation**refers to the process of acquiring and increasing the number of people with skills, good health, education and experience that are critical for economic growth.** Option I is correct. Expenditure on education is the primary source of human capital.**It directly enhances the supply of a skilled and educated labour force.** Option II is incorrect. **The investment made on the purchase of machines and equipment leads to**physical capital **formation or fixed capital formation.**Physical capital consists of**tangible assets**such as buildings, machinery and equipment used in the production of goods and services.**Such expenditure does not directly contribute to human **capital formation**, as it does not enhance the knowledge, skills, health or abilities of individuals.** Option III is incorrect. **Expenditure made on providing pensions to old age people is a type of**social welfare **expenditure**. It**does not directly enhance**the productive capacity of human resources.** Option IV is correct. **Firms incur costs for providing on-the-job training to workers. The returns from such expenditure is in the form of enhanced labour productivity.**So, it is considered as a source of Human Capital **formation. **Expenditure regarding on-the-job training is a source of human capital.
Human Capital formation as the return of such expenditure in the form of enhanced labour productivity is more than the cost of it.

questions.questionText: The Brundtland Commission Report, often cited in global environmental discourse, is primarily associated with:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **

Option a is incorrect: Ozone layer protection: **This is mainly addressed by the**Vienna Convention (1985)**and the**Montreal Protocol (1987),**which focus on controlling substances that deplete the ozone layer. The Brundtland Report did not specifically deal with ozone issues.**

Option b is correct**: The**Brundtland Commission**, formally known as the**World Commission on Environment and **Development**(WCED), was**established in 1983 by the United Nations**, to examine the interlinkages between environment and economic development. The Commission released its report**Our Common Future in 1987**, which popularised the concept of sustainable development, defined as “development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. The report emphasised the integration of economic growth, environmental protection, and social equity, laying the foundation for subsequent global environmental governance.**

Option c is incorrect: Climate change mitigation: **Global measures for climate change were emphasized later under the**UNFCCC (1992) and Kyoto Protocol (1997).**The Brundtland Report focused on sustainable development broadly, not specific climate mitigation targets. 

Option d is incorrect: Biodiversity conservation: **Biodiversity was formally addressed under the**Convention on **Biological Diversity (CBD, 1992**). While the Brundtland Report discussed environmental protection, it did not primarily focus on biodiversity.

statementLines: Consider the following statements about the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR):
I. It was introduced by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs as part of its corporate social responsibility norms.
II. Presently, the top 1,000 listed companies in India in terms of their market capitalisation are mandated to report
Business Responsibility Reports (BRR) as part of their annual report.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR): I. It was introduced by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs as part of its corporate social responsibility norms. II. Presently, the top 1,000 listed companies in India in terms of their market capitalisation are mandated to report Business Responsibility Reports (BRR) as part of their annual report. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **The**Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report**(BRSR) is a**comprehensive Environmental, Social, and **Governance (ESG) disclosure framework.**It was introduced with the objective of enhancing transparency, accountability, and standardisation in sustainability reporting by listed companies. BRSR**aligns corporate reporting in India with global ESG practices**and provides stakeholders including investors, regulators, and the public with reliable, comparable information on a company’s environmental performance, social responsibilities, and governance standards.**

Statement I is incorrect:**The**Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report**(BRSR) was**not introduced by the **Ministry of Corporate Affairs**(MCA). Instead,**it was introduced by the Securities and Exchange Board of India**(SEBI)**in 2021**as part of effort to strengthen and standardise**Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) disclosures**by listed companies. SEBI replaced the earlier**Business Responsibility Report**(BRR) with BRSR to bring India’s reporting standards closer to global ESG frameworks. 

Statement II is correct: **In May 2021, SEBI introduced a new ESG reporting structure titled ‘Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting’ to make**it mandatory for the top 1000 listed companies in the stock exchange (by **market capitalisation)**, to report Business Responsibility Reports (BRR) as part of their annual report from**FY 2022 – **2023** onwards and maintain transparency with their key stakeholders.
The objective is to ensure standardized, comparable, and comprehensive reporting on ESG parameters, enhancing transparency for investors and stakeholders.

**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojana (PMSSY) was announced in 2003 with objectives of correcting regional imbalances in the availability of affordable/ reliable tertiary healthcare services and also to augment facilities for quality medical education in the country.**

Statement 1 is correct**: The PMSSY seeks to**address the disparities in the distribution of advanced healthcare **facilities across different regions of the country**. Statement 3 is correct: **One of the key objectives of the PMSSY is to**enhance the infrastructure and facilities for **medical education in India. **This includes setting up new medical colleges, increasing the number of seats for undergraduate and postgraduate medical courses, and improving the quality of medical education.

questions.questionText: Consider the following functions: I. Registration and Regulation of all the trade unions. II. Conducting Periodic Labour Force Survey. III. Compilation and Maintenance of Consumer Price Index for Industrial Workers. How many of the above are the functions of the Labour bureau in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The Labour Bureau is an attached office of the Ministry of Labour & Employment which is headed by the Director General, a Higher Administrative Grade Officer from the Indian Economic Service (IES). It performs various functions under five major heads: (i) Labour Intelligence, (ii) Labour Research, (iii) Monitoring and evaluation, (iv) Regular Publications, (v) Training.** Option I is incorrect:**According to Section 3 of the Trade Union Act of 1926, the appropriate government shall **appoint a person to be Registrar of Trade Unions for each state. Here appropriate government can be centre or **state. **Labour bureau does not have a role in the registration or regulation of trade union. ** Option II is incorrect:**The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) is conducted by National Statistical Organisation **(not Labour Bureau) **functioning under Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation. It acts as the primary source of data on activity participation and employment-unemployment conditions of the population.** Option III is correct**:**The Labour Bureau is the central agency responsible for the collection, collation, and **dissemination of labour, employment, and price statistics in India**, and it serves as a major source of primary data on several key economic indicators.**Its functions include compiling the**Consumer Price Index for Industrial Workers **(CPI-IW)**, as well as CPI for Agricultural and Rural Labourers, Wage Rate Indices.

Global Hunger Index United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) II. World Happiness Report UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN) III. World Inequality Report World Economic Forum (WEF) IV. World Migration Report International Organisation for Migration How many of the statements given above are correct?

**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Pair I is incorrect: The Global Hunger Index (GHI) is jointly released by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerhilfe, **not by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).**It serves as a comprehensive tool to assess and track hunger at global, regional, and national levels, using four key indicators—undernourishment, child stunting, child wasting, and child mortality. In the 2025 edition of the Global Hunger Index, India is ranked 102nd out of 123 countries with a score of 25.8, placing it in the category of nations with a serious level of hunger.**Pair II is correct:**The World Happiness Report is released by the United Nations Sustainable Development **Network in partnership with the Wellbeing Research Centre at Oxford University. India's rank in the 2025 World **Happiness Report is 118th out of 147 countries. **Pair III is incorrect: The World Inequality Report is released by World Inequality Lab. **The 2026 report is the third in the series after earlier 2018 and 2022 editions. As per the report, Income inequality in India remains among the highest in the world, with the top 10 per cent of earners capturing 58 per cent of national income.**Pair IV is correct: International Organisation For Migration has been publishing its flagship report every 2 years **since 2000 , with latest World Migration Report 2024**. It states India is the largest contributor to international migrants , contributing 18 million diaspora across the world with major destinations being US, UAE, Saudi Arabia etc.
Besides, India also ranks first in terms for remittances received followed by Mexico , and China.

questions.questionText: In context to poverty estimation in India, consider the following: I. The poverty line should be based on the Mixed Reference Period. II. There shall be a uniform Poverty Line Basket across rural and urban areas. III. The poverty line shall incorporate private expenditure on health and education. The above-mentioned recommendations were made by which one of the following Committees?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **In 2009, the Tendulkar Committee recommended several changes in the methodology of poverty estimation in India. It recommended four major changes: (i) a shift away from calorie consumption based poverty estimation; (ii) a uniform poverty line basket (PLB) across rural and urban India**; (iii) a change in the price adjustment procedure to correct spatial and temporal issues with price adjustment; and (iv)**incorporation of private expenditure on health **and education while estimating poverty**. The Committee recommended using**Mixed Reference Period (MRP) **based estimates, as opposed to Uniform Reference Period (URP) based estimates that were used in earlier methods for estimating poverty.

Recognising its social and economic relevance, the Ministry of Women and Child Development (MWCD) launched the Poshan Bhi Padhai Bhi initiative to transform Anganwadi Centres into vibrant early learning hubs.**To support this transformation, MWCD **has introduced Aadharshila and Navchetna, which serve as national frameworks and curricula aimed at **strengthening and standardizing ECCE across the country**.
Aadharshila is the National Curriculum for ECCE that focuses on the holistic development of children by nurturing their intellectual, emotional, physical, and social well-being through structured, play-based learning. statementLines: Consider the following statements:
Statement I: Circular economy reduces the emissions of greenhouse gases.
Statement II: Circular economy reduces the use of raw materials as inputs.
Statement III: Circular economy reduces wastage in the production process.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Circular economy reduces the emissions of greenhouse gases. Statement II: Circular economy reduces the use of raw materials as inputs. Statement III: Circular economy reduces wastage in the production process. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is Correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The**circular economy **is a model of production and consumption that emphasizes sharing, leasing, reusing, repairing, refurbishing, and recycling existing materials and products for as long as possible. This approach helps create a closed-loop system that minimizes waste and reduces the need for new resource extraction.**

Statement I is correct: **According to the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) Sixth Assessment Report (2023), human activities such as burning fossil fuels, deforestation, and intensive agriculture have contributed significantly to global warming, leading to an estimated 1.1°C increase in average temperature since the early 20th century.**A circular economy mitigates these impacts by reducing waste, lowering resource consumption, and **decreasing greenhouse gas emissions through sustainable practices. **

Statement II is correct: **With the global population increasing, so does the demand for raw materials. However, many essential natural resources are finite.**The circular economy reduces dependence on virgin materials by promoting **the reuse and recycling of products, **conserving resources, and protecting ecosystems, thereby supporting biodiversity.**

Statement III is correct: The circular economy also targets waste reduction during production by promoting **efficient design and resource reuse**. For example, agricultural waste like animal manure is repurposed into organic fertilizer and biogas, which supports sustainable farming and energy use. As circular economy helps to mitigate greenhouse gas emissions through low raw material consumption and low waste production,**option a is the correct **answer: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I **

**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Sovereign Green Bonds**(SGrBs) are**government-issued debt instruments**specifically designed to mobilize resources for environmentally sustainable and climate-positive projects.**In 2022**, the**Government of India released the Sovereign Green Bond Framework**, which provides the guiding principles for the issuance, allocation, management, and reporting of these bonds. The Framework applies to all sovereign Green Bonds issued by the Government of India.**

Statement I is incorrect:**SGrBs aims to use the funds for green infrastructure investments, thereby reducing the Indian economy's carbon intensity.** These bonds usually offer lower interest rates compared to the conventional **government securities (G-Secs)**.**

statementLines: In the context of Indian health sector between the years 2014-15 and 2021-22, consider the following statements:
Statement I: The out-of-pocket expenditure as a percentage of total health expenditure in India has declined.
Statement II: The Government health expenditure as a percentage of total health expenditure has increased.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: In the context of Indian health sector between the years 2014-15 and 2021-22, consider the following statements: Statement I: The out-of-pocket expenditure as a percentage of total health expenditure in India has declined. Statement II: The Government health expenditure as a percentage of total health expenditure has increased. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct, but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct, but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **

Statement I is correct: **Between 2015 and 2022, India’s out-of-pocket expenditure (OOPE) on health declined significantly. OOPE as a share of Total Health Expenditure fell from around**62.6% in FY15 to 39.4% in FY22**, and OOPE as a percentage of GDP also reduced from**2.43% to 1.51%**. This indicates that households were spending a smaller portion of their income directly on medical care, reflecting a meaningful reduction in financial burden.**

Statement II is correct: **During the same period, Government Health Expenditure (GHE) increased steadily. GHE as a share of Total Health Expenditure rose from**29% in FY15 to 48% in FY22**, and government spending as a share of GDP increased from**1.1% to 1.84%**. Higher public spending was channeled into primary health services, social health insurance schemes like AB-PMJAY, and capital investments in health infrastructure, strengthening the public health system.**Statement II explains Statement I: According to the Economic Survey the increase in public health spending has a **direct role in reducing household financial hardship**. As the government’s share in health financing expanded, the reliance on out-of-pocket payments by households decreased proportionally. Higher GHE—through free essential services, expanded insurance cover, and subsidised programmes reduced the need for families to pay from their own pockets.

It provides a cover of ₹5 lakh per year for primary healthcare across the empanelled hospitals in India. II. It is a Central Sector Scheme. III. All senior citizens aged 70 or above, regardless of economic status, are eligible for free medical treatment up to ₹5 lakh under this scheme IV. It covers pre-existing diseases from the first day of enrollment. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Ayushman Bharat, launched as per the National Health Policy 2017, advances India’s goal of**Universal Health **Coverage**and aligns with the SDGs’ pledge to “leave no one behind.” It shifts healthcare from a fragmented to a comprehensive model, addressing prevention, promotion, and treatment across all levels. The scheme follows a**continuum of care**through two components:**Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs)**and the**Pradhan Mantri Jan **Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)**.**

Statement I is incorrect: **PM-JAY provides**₹5 lakh per family per year**,**for secondary and tertiary care**. It provides a cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization across public and private empanelled hospitals in India. Primary healthcare is covered under a different component of Ayushman Bharat in the**Health and Wellness Centres **(HWCs)**.**

Statement II is incorrect: **PM-JAY is**not**a Central Sector Scheme. It is a**Centrally Sponsored Scheme**, meaning the**Centre and States share funding**(60:40 for most states, 90:10 for North Eastern/hilly states, 100% for UTs without legislature).**

Statement III is correct: **All senior citizens aged 70 or above, regardless of economic status, are eligible for free medical treatment up to ₹5 lakh under this scheme**

Statement IV is correct: **PM-JAY**covers all pre-existing diseases from the first day of enrolment**, with no waiting period. This ensures immediate access to treatment for chronic conditions.

Knowledge Base: The households included are based on the deprivation and occupational criteria of the Socio-Economic Caste Census 2011 (SECC 2011) for rural and urban areas respectively.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **

Statement 1 is incorrect:**Prophylactic Iron Folic Acid supplementation (not Prophylactic calcium)**given to children, adolescents, women of reproductive age and pregnant women is one of the key continued interventions given under Anaemia Mukt Bharat.**

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following best describes the situation of Population Momentum? replacement level fertility. higher than the replacement level fertility. the country remains unchanged.
questions.questionText: Which of the following best describes the situation of Population Momentum? replacement level fertility. higher than the replacement level fertility. the country remains unchanged.

options.a: A country experiencing a rise in population for decades even after the fertility rate in the country reaches below the
options.b: A country sustaining a significant rise in the population as the fertility rate for the last few decades has been much
options.c: A country experiencing an increase in population mainly because the mortality rate declines while fertility rate in
options.d: A country exhibiting an increase in population primarily due to in-migration from other countries.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **

Option a is correct:**A country experiences** Population momentum**when**fertility falls to or below the replacement **level**, yet the**population continues to grow**because a**large proportion of people are in the younger age group**. As these young individuals enter the reproductive age, they will have children of their own, and the**number of births will **remain high**even if fertility per woman is low. This means that**the sheer size of the young population**entering childbearing years will**outnumber the total deaths**, keeping the population increasing for several years or decades. Thus, even with low fertility, population growth continues due to the**age structure **

Option b is incorrect: **Population momentum does**not require fertility to remain above replacement level**. In fact, momentum operates**even when fertility has already fallen to replacement or below**, as the age structure (youth bulge) drives growth, not high fertility across all age groups.**

Option c is incorrect: **A decline in mortality with unchanged fertility can cause population growth, but this describes**early demographic transition**, not population momentum. Momentum specifically occurs**after fertility decline**, not merely due to falling death rates.**

Option d is incorrect: **Population momentum is**not primarily driven by in-migration**. InMigration can influence population size, but momentum arises from**internal age structure dynamics**. For example, countries like the**USA **and Western Europe**have experienced population increases due to**immigration of younger working-age **populations**, but this is**migration-led growth**, not population momentum in the demographic sense.

statementLines: With reference to the Viksit Bharat—Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB—G RAM G) Act,
2025, consider the following statements:
I. It guarantees at least 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household.
II. As per the act the entire cost of wages is borne by the Central Government.
III. Social audit is mandatory for all works carried out under the mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Viksit Bharat—Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB—G RAM G) Act, 2025, consider the following statements: I. It guarantees at least 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household. II. As per the act the entire cost of wages is borne by the Central Government. III. Social audit is mandatory for all works carried out under the mission. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The**Viksit Bharat—Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) act , 2025**, replaced MGNREGA, aligning rural employment with long term vision of Vikshit Bharat 2047. It increased guaranteed annual rural employment to 125 days. It shifts the 100% central wage funding to a shared Centre-State model (mostly 60:40) and integrates planning with PM Gati Shakti.**

Statement I is incorrect: **The**VB—G RAM G Act, 2025 increases the guaranteed employment from 100 days to 125 **days**per rural household in a financial year.**

Statement II is incorrect: As per the act**, the**entire wage cost is not borne by the Central Government**. The scheme is converted into a**centrally sponsored scheme**with a**60:40 cost-sharing ratio**between the Centre and States (and**90:10**for North-Eastern and Himalayan states).**

Statement III is correct: Social audit remains mandatory**under the VB—G RAM G framework. The act strengthens the role of**Gram Sabhas**, mandates periodic social audits (at least once every six months), and integrates them into the transparency and accountability framework. 

Knowledge Base: **Salient features of Vikshit Bharat G-RAM-G ACT 2025 are i) Enhanced employment guarantee-wage employment increased from from 100 to 125 days per household. ii)Focused nature of works with 4 priority areas-water security, rural infrastructure, livelihood infrastructure and climate resilience. iii) Support to agriculture-state governments can notify pause in work for a total of 60 days(per year) during a peak sowing and harvesting season to ensure labour availability. iv) Strong transparency and oversight-AI based monitoring, real time dashboards and mandatory social audits. v) Predictive funding framework-shift towards normative funding from demand based funding thus ensuring predictable budgeting while also protecting employment guarantee.
